title: fix(ci): clean working tree before switching to apidocs-sync branch
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
The `Regenerate API Docs` workflow has been failing at the `Create or update docs PR` step with:

```
error: Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by checkout:
	APIDOCS.md
```

The step regenerates `APIDOCS.md` on the `main` checkout, copies it to `/tmp/APIDOCS.md.new`, then runs `git checkout -B docs/apidocs-sync origin/docs/apidocs-sync`. When the remote sync branch's `APIDOCS.md` differs from `main`'s, git refuses the checkout to avoid clobbering the uncommitted regeneration.

Fix: `git checkout -- APIDOCS.md` right after the `/tmp` copy, so the working tree is clean before the branch switch. The freshly generated content is re-applied from `/tmp/APIDOCS.md.new` after checkout, as before.

Failing run: https://github.com/pollinations/pollinations/actions/runs/25961789713

gist.headline: Clean tree, happy CI
gist.blurb: Fixed a snag in our automated API docs pipeline so the robots can get back to writing our manuals without tripping over their own files.
gist.summary: Resolved a git checkout conflict in the API docs regeneration workflow by cleaning the working tree before switching to the sync branch.
gist.impact: Automated API documentation updates will now successfully create PRs without failing on uncommitted changes to APIDOCS.md.
gist.visual_concept: A chunky robot sweeping up messy papers (uncommitted changes) to clean up a workspace, while the bee holds a fresh, clean 'DOCS' manual.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head sweeping up messy papers from the floor with a broom. The cute bee mascot hovers nearby holding a pristine, glowing book. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
